rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M1 '## Homework after Module 1, between-module reading' (Cherny video + multi-session ref) mirrored in M2 Prework: 'Optional reading in the Module 1 to Module 2 gap: Boris Cherny... Multi-session and Git survival guide.' Symmetric duplication.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts explicit at both ends
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: ./CLAUDE.local.md named on producing side (M1 Ex3 compound prompt; maintainer-block contract row) and consuming side (M2 Next 'CLAUDE.local.md sits at the top of the next session').
M1 Next: 'Close this session when the ticket lands; Module 2 opens fresh in the same repo.' M2 Prework: 'in the repo you used for Module 1.' Same-repo continuation explicit.
M1 send-off is the closed ticket + PR — not a long autonomous run that could be thin/missing for M2. M2 load-bears on ./CLAUDE.local.md which student authored interactively, not autonomously.
M2 Prework: 'Come to Module 2 without a surfaced task and you'll be scrambling to find one while the room is already in plan mode. Your call.' Canonical form, no module-internal vocabulary leak.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Peek at the next module before adding a second exercise

rules_evaluated.fix_hint: M2 '## Pre-reads for Module 3' (Willison lethal-trifecta + OWASP) mirrored in M3 Prework: 'Optional pre-reads in the Module 2 to Module 3 gap: Simon Willison... OWASP Top 10...' Same links + one-line framing in both places.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts explicit at both ends
rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 Next line 69: 'Ask Claude where it wrote the plan file, note the path; M3 reads it.' M3 Prework line 12: 'The plan file Claude Code wrote during plan mode is what M3 reads (you noted the path at M2 close).' Plan-file identifier is student-discovered (`ask Claude where it wrote`), not a stable path the producing prompt guarantees. The receiving module assumes the artefact exists by an identifier the producing side did not pin.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either pin plan-file path in M2 producing prompt (have Claude write to a named location like `./plan-<task-slug>.md` or `.specstory/plans/<task>.md`), OR re-frame M3 as 'paste the plan content from M2 scrollback' so the artefact lives in conversation, not on disk. Then drop the path-noting move.

M2 Next: 'Close this session when the second read lands. M3 opens fresh and runs security skills against the M2 feature.' M3 Prework: 'M3 continues in the same repo as M1 and M2. The session opens at the first exercise.' Same-repo continuation + fresh-session both ends. M3 'Sharpen the skill from evidence' also names m3-security vs m3-quality windows explicitly.

rules_evaluated.evidence: M2 maintainer-block Artefact-contracts table (lines 131-134) lists Task-shaping rule file + Personal rules update. Plan file produced in M2 plan mode + consumed by M3 has no contract row. Per §5 carve-out 'artefacts the student carries entirely in scrollback (no path) are exempt — only file / branch artefacts trigger the contract requirement' — but M2 explicitly tells the student to note the path, so it's a file artefact, not scrollback.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add an Artefact-contracts row to M2 maintainer block for the plan file: stable identifier (or 'student-noted path'), produced by push-back-on-the-plan exercise, consumed by M3 Prework + M3 first exercise. Or resolve §3 first by pinning the producing-side path, then add the row with that identifier.

M2's send-off is the plan-approval + opportunistic rule save — not an autonomous run that could be thin/missing. M3 load-bears on the feature itself (carried as recognition + scrollback), not on an artefact from a long unattended run. Rule fires on M5/M6 send-off shapes, not M2→M3.

M2→M3 has 'Bring the feature you planned at M2, that's the default' framing in M3 Prework but no canonical 'Come to Module 3 without X and you'll be scrambling for Y' stakes line. M2's Pre-reads are explicitly 'Optional' so no stake to name. M3's Prework names the bring instruction without the stakes-line shape — borderline absent rather than malformed. Rule §8 fires on SHAPE of stakes lines when present; not on obligation to have one at every boundary. Consider adding a stakes line at M3 Prework: 'Come to Module 3 without a feature with an external or user-facing surface and you'll be scrambling for one while the room is already mapping access. Your call.' — but the absence isn't a §8 violation as written.
